name: removed_value_still_present
code:
proof fn phi_1_removed_value_still_present<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail != remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_head != remove_index,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self@.len() == old_self@.len() - 1,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
    ensures
        post_self@.contains(ret),
{
}
reason: If the removed value is still present after removal, the remove operation is semantically broken

name: length_drops_to_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_length_drops_to_zero<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail != remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_head != remove_index,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self@.len() == old_self@.len() - 1,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
    ensures
        post_self@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Precondition requires value_list_len != 1 (so len >= 2), meaning post-length must be >= 1, never 0

name: other_element_lost
code:
proof fn phi_3_other_element_lost<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
    x: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail != remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_head != remove_index,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self@.len() == old_self@.len() - 1,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
        old_self@.contains(x),
        x != ret,
    ensures
        !post_self@.contains(x),
{
}
reason: An element distinct from the removed one should be preserved; losing it means removal is destructive beyond its target

name: sequence_unchanged_after_remove
code:
proof fn phi_4_sequence_unchanged_after_remove<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail != remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_head != remove_index,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self@.len() == old_self@.len() - 1,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
    ensures
        post_self@ =~= old_self@,
{
}
reason: If the sequence is identical before and after removal, the function had no observable effect on the abstract state

name: node_ref_changes_for_remaining
code:
proof fn phi_5_node_ref_changes_for_remaining<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
    x: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail != remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_head != remove_index,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self@.len() == old_self@.len() - 1,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
        forall|w: T| #![auto] post_self@.contains(w) ==> old_self.get_node_ref(w) == post_self.get_node_ref(w),
        post_self@.contains(x),
    ensures
        old_self.get_node_ref(x) != post_self.get_node_ref(x),
{
}
reason: The spec guarantees node refs are stable for surviving elements; if this proves, the stability postcondition is contradictory
